title: Defining dock layouts
text: Internally, every board carries dock_layouts collection. named list one views (global tabs see top app). view’s content dock_layout: per-view panel arrangement, stored tree block / extension IDs. Panel content derived board’s blocks extensions demand — arrangement stored. Single-page boards degenerate case: dock_layouts one auto-named view called "Page". view-nav dropdown always present; just one entry add . pass layouts = argument new_dock_board(), constructor normalises dock_layouts:  dispatch name-driven: list top-level names becomes multi-view (one slot per name); unnamed list (dock_layout) treated single-page grid spec. get multiple views, name top-level entries. ever need think two things: grid syntax arranging panels inside view, named-list syntax one view.

title: Defining dock layouts
text: simplest possible board: blocks, extensions, layouts. Since new_dock_board() defaults layouts = default_layout(blocks, extensions) — returns empty dock_layout — empty layout gets wrapped single Page view, resulting collection dock_layouts one empty Page slot: get single auto-named Page view holding watermark prompt. Clicking Add panel normally open panel picker, board blocks extensions choose , picker says points add block first. empty starting point can grow board interactively. supply blocks extensions still layouts, default Page auto-populated default_layout(): blocks alone become single row panels; blocks together extensions become familiar sidebar--main shape (extensions left, blocks right). Pass layouts = override .

title: Defining dock layouts
text: simplest layout: list (character vector) block/extension IDs. shape list determines grid. two rules internalise: List nesting alternates orientation. top level lays children horizontally; one level nesting flips vertical; another level flips back horizontal, . Character vectors create tabs. vector IDs lives inside one DockView panel; list IDs gets split multiple panels.

title: Defining dock layouts
text: single ID gives one panel filling whole view:

title: Defining dock layouts
text: Two top-level entries → two columns split horizontally:

title: Defining dock layouts
text: Wrap entries one extra layer list() introduce vertical split: outer list still describes horizontal split, one child “split” single full-width column. inner list("", "b") depth 1, splits vertically: stacks top b.

title: Defining dock layouts
text: Use character vector (list) put multiple panels DockView panel tabs: list("", "b") (panels split) list(c("", "b")) (panels tabbed) look almost identical source produce different UIs. list/vector distinction flips split panel tabify panel.

title: Defining dock layouts
text: Combine two rules build layout.

what: Two columns, the right one stacked
title: Defining dock layouts
Defining dock layouts
text: Add third level nesting flip back horizontal inside vertical stack. Useful want row holds two panels side--side:

title: Defining dock layouts
text: common dashboard shape: narrow column left holding extension, tabbed panel right several blocks: also default_layout() produces blocks extensions present: extensions left, blocks right.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   extensions = new_edit_board_extension(),   layouts = list("edit_board_extension", c("a", "b")) ) ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Page  [+]                 │ ├─────────┬──────────────────┤ │         │ ┌────┐┌────┐     │ │  edit   │ │ a  ││ b  │     │ │         │ └────┘└────┘     │ │         │                  │ │         │   (tabs)         │ └─────────┴──────────────────┘

title: Defining dock layouts
text: Pass named list. named entry becomes separate page view-nav dropdown: Analysis view (active default, since first view wins): Switching Editor via dropdown: slot value follows exactly grid syntax single-page form: character vectors tabs, nested lists splits.

title: Defining dock layouts
text: default, first layout active. start different one, name new_dock_board(active = ), using view id (layouts key): view active tracked single field dock_layouts collection, keyed view id — like id , belongs collection, one layout. always exactly one: id named active (defaulting first view). Change runtime active_view(board) <- id.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   extensions = new_edit_board_extension(),   layouts = list(     Analysis = list("a", "b"),     Editor = dock_layout("edit_board_extension")   ),   active = "Editor" ) ┌────────────────────────────┐ │ Editor  [+]   ← starts here│ ├────────────────────────────┤ │           edit             │ └────────────────────────────┘

title: Defining dock layouts
text: view panels fine. Switching shows watermark prompt empty default board (see Starting scratch), scoped tab.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   layouts = list(     Analysis = list("a", "b"),     Empty = list()   ) )

title: Defining dock layouts
text: pot-pourri exercising every feature: multiple views, nested grids, tabbed panels, extension sidebar, explicit active view. board three views. Charts marked active, user lands first. Charts (active load): one tabbed panel plot1 shown plot2 selectable tab. Data: slim extension sidebar left holding editor, wide right column raw / cleaned tabbed. active = "cleaned" opens cleaned tab default; sizes = c(0.25, 0.75) carves narrow sidebar. Analysis: two top-level columns 55/45. left column nested group() 40/60 vertical split (summary top, plot1 ); right column single panel (plot2).
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(     raw = new_dataset_block(),     cleaned = new_head_block(),     summary = new_head_block(),     plot1 = new_scatter_block(),     plot2 = new_scatter_block()   ),   extensions = new_edit_board_extension(),   links = list(     new_link("raw", "cleaned", "data"),     new_link("cleaned", "summary", "data"),     new_link("cleaned", "plot1", "data"),     new_link("cleaned", "plot2", "data")   ),   layouts = list(     Data = dock_layout(       "edit_board_extension",       panels("raw", "cleaned", active = "cleaned"),       sizes = c(0.25, 0.75)     ),     Analysis = dock_layout(       group("summary", "plot1", sizes = c(0.4, 0.6)),       "plot2",       sizes = c(0.55, 0.45)     ),     Charts = dock_layout(panels("plot1", "plot2"))   ),   active = "Charts" ) ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Charts  [+]               │ ├────────────────────────────┤ │ ┌───────┐┌───────┐         │ │ │ plot1 ││ plot2 │         │ │ └───────┘└───────┘         │ │                            │ │  (plot1 shown, plot2 tab)  │ │                            │ └────────────────────────────┘ ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Data  [+]                 │ ├──────┬─────────────────────┤ │      │┌─────┐┌─────────┐   │ │ edit ││ raw ││ cleaned │   │ │      │└─────┘└─────────┘   │ │      │  (cleaned shown)    │ └──────┴─────────────────────┘   25%          75% ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Analysis  [+]             │ ├─────────────┬──────────────┤ │   summary   │              │   40% / 55% ├─────────────┤    plot2     │ │             │              │ │    plot1    │              │   60% / 45% │             │              │ └─────────────┴──────────────┘      55%             45%

what: Custom split ratios and active tabs
title: Defining dock layouts
text: list--IDs sugar splits space evenly opens first panel tabbed group. non-default ratios non-default open tab, escalate typed constructors: dock_layout(..., sizes = c(...)) — sizes parallel root children. numbers relative; don’t sum 1. group(..., sizes = c(...)) — , nested branch (level root). panels(..., active = "...") — tabbed leaf explicit open tab. Equivalent character-vector child, except also get pick active tab.

previous_headings: Custom split ratios and active tabs
title: Defining dock layouts
text: dock_layout(..., sizes =) runs parallel children passed .... two children layout splits horizontally; two sizes unevenly:

previous_headings: Custom split ratios and active tabs
title: Defining dock layouts
text: idea, vertical layout: pass orientation = "vertical" root split runs top--bottom instead left--right.

previous_headings: Custom split ratios and active tabs
title: Defining dock layouts
text: panels() builds tabbed leaf. Without active, first ID opens default — behaviour bare character vector. Pass active open different tab load:

previous_headings: Custom split ratios and active tabs
title: Defining dock layouts
text: group() equivalent inner list(...) sizes. Use whenever need ratios non-root branch: Outer sizes = c(0.3, 0.7) controls root split (left / right); inner group(..., sizes = c(0.6, 0.4)) controls right column’s internal stack.

title: Defining dock layouts
text: See ?dock_layout per-view layout type (including panels() group()) ?active_view switching active view runtime.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Nicolas Bennett. Author, maintainer. David Granjon. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bennett N, Granjon D (2026). blockr.dock: Docking Layout Manager 'blockr'. R package version 0.1.2, https://bristolmyerssquibb.github.io/blockr.dock/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {blockr.dock: A Docking Layout Manager for 'blockr'},   author = {Nicolas Bennett and David Granjon},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.2},   url = {https://bristolmyerssquibb.github.io/blockr.dock/}, }

what: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: docking layout manager provided dockViewR can used front-end blockr board using package.

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: can install development version blockr.dock GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("BristolMyersSquibb/blockr.dock")

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: start board visualizing Sepal.Length Sepal.Width iris dataset: Simple dock
code: library(blockr.dock) library(blockr.core)  serve(   new_dock_board(     blocks = c(       a = new_dataset_block("iris"),       b = new_scatter_block(x = "Sepal.Length", y = "Sepal.Width")     ),     links = list(from = "a", to = "b", input = "data"),     extensions = list(edit = new_edit_board_extension()),     layouts = list("edit", list("a", "b"))   ) )

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: locked dock prevents users adding removing blocks extensions. Drag--drop panel resizing also disabled. Locked dock
code: library(blockr.dock) library(blockr.core)  options(blockr.dock_is_locked = TRUE)  serve(   new_dock_board(     blocks = c(       a = new_dataset_block("iris"),       b = new_head_block(n = 20L),       c = new_scatter_block(x = "Sepal.Length", y = "Sepal.Width"),       d = new_subset_block()     ),     links = c(       new_link("a", "b", input = "data"),       new_link("b", "c", input = "data"),       new_link("b", "d", input = "data")     ),     layouts = list(list("a", "b"), list("c", "d"))   ) )

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: Since blockr.dock 0.1.1 every board carries dock_layouts object: list one views, exposed tab dropdown top app. single-page case (rendered -tab dock 0.1.0) now dock_layouts one auto-named "Page" view, see Simple dock screenshot . define multiple views explicitly, pass named list layouts. named entry becomes tab; blocks extensions shared across views via board’s DAG, view membership layout concern . Multi-view dock full set shapes accepted new_dock_board(layouts = ...) (nested grids, tabbed panels, active-view selection, coercion rules normalise everything dock_layouts), see vignette("layouts", package = "blockr.dock").
code: library(blockr.core) library(blockr.dock)  board <- new_dock_board(   extensions = blockr.dag::new_dag_extension(),   blocks = c(     dataset_1 = new_dataset_block(),     head_1 = new_head_block()   ),   links = new_link("dataset_1", "head_1"),   layouts = list(     Analysis = dock_layout(       "dag_extension",       panels("dataset_1", "head_1", active = "head_1"),       sizes = c(0.3, 0.7)     ),     Overview = dock_layout("dag_extension"),     Empty = list()   ),   active = "Overview" )  serve(board, "my_board")

title: Board actions — new_action
text: Logic including modal-based UI board actions "append block" "edit stack" can specified using action objects, essentially classed shiny server functions.

title: Board actions — new_action
code: new_action(func, id)  is_action(x)  is_action_generator(x)  action_id(x)  board_actions(x, ...)  action_triggers(x)  block_input_select(   block = NULL,   block_id = NULL,   links = NULL,   mode = c("create", "update", "inputs"),   ... )  block_registry_selectize(id, blocks = list_blocks())  board_select(id, blocks, selected = NULL, ...)

title: Board actions — new_action
text: func function used create shiny::moduleServer(). id Input ID x Object ... Forwarded methods block Block object block_id Block ID links Links object mode Switch determining return object blocks Character vector block registry IDs selected Character vector pre-selected block (registry) IDs

title: Board actions — new_action
text: constructor new_action returns classed function inherits action. Inheritance can checked functions is_action(), is_action_generator() checks whether objects function returns action object. String-value action IDs can retrieved action_id() set actions associated board can enumerated via board_actions(). Finally, action_triggers() returns named list objects suitable use action triggers. utilities block_input_select(), block_registry_selectize() board_select, see respective sections.

title: Board actions — new_action
text: action function can called arguments input, output session, behaving one expect shiny server module function. Actions typically created action generator functions, unique ID shiny::reactiveVal()-based trigger object (inheriting action_trigger). Action trigger objects implement counter-based invalidation mechanism (top reactive values behave).

title: Board actions — new_action
text: Determine input options block removing inputs already used also takes account edge-cases, variadic blocks. mode set "inputs", return character vector, "create", return value shiny::selectizeInput() call "update", return value shiny::updateSelectizeInput() call.

title: Board actions — new_action
text: creates UI block registry selector via shiny::selectizeInput() returns object inherits shiny.tag.

title: Board actions — new_action
text: Block selection UI, enumerating blocks board available board_select(). object inherits shiny.tag returned, contains result shiny::selectizeInput() call.

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
text: Using docking layout manager provided dockViewR, dock_board extends blockr.core::new_board(). addition attributes contained core board, also includes dock extensions (extensions) panel arrangement (layouts). layouts field always stored internally dock_layouts collection (multi-view); single-page boards degenerate case one auto-named "Page" view.

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = list(),   links = list(),   stacks = list(),   ...,   extensions = new_dock_extensions(),   layouts = default_layout(blocks, extensions),   active = NULL,   options = dock_board_options(),   ctor = NULL,   pkg = NULL,   class = character() )  is_dock_board(x)  as_dock_board(x, ...)  active_layout(x)  active_layout(x) <- value  dock_extensions(x)  dock_extensions(x) <- value  dock_ext_ids(x)  dock_board_options()

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
text: blocks Set blocks links Set links stacks Set stacks ... (metadata) attributes extensions Dock extensions layouts named list per-view arrangements (multi-view), dock_layout / raw list (single-page), existing dock_layouts collection. forms normalised dock_layouts. active Id initially active view (key layouts). Defaults first view. view active property collection, individual layout. options Board-level user settings ctor, pkg Constructor information (used serialization) class Board sub-class x Board object value Replacement value

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
text: constructor new_dock_board() returns board object, coercion function as_dock_board(). Inheritance can checked using is_dock_board(), returns boolean. board_layouts() returns board's dock_layouts; active_layout() returns active view's dock_layout active_layout<-() writes active view. dock_extensions() dock_extensions<-() accessors return / set board's dock_extension objects. character vector IDs returned dock_ext_ids() dock_board_options() returns board_options object.

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
text: multi-view boards, pass named list layouts = — name becomes view, value panel arrangement (dock_layout() raw list block / extension IDs). single-page board, pass dock_layout raw list directly. Either way input normalised dock_layouts, leaf IDs resolved board's blocks extensions.

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
code: brd <- new_dock_board(c(a = blockr.core::new_dataset_block())) str(active_layout(brd), max.level = 2) #>  <dock_layout> a

code: new_dock_extension(   server,   ui,   name,   class,   description = NULL,   ctor = sys.parent(),   pkg = NULL,   options = new_board_options(),   external_ctrl = FALSE,   ... )  is_dock_extension(x)  validate_extension(x, ...)  extension_ui(x, id, ...)  extension_server(x, ...)  extension_id(x)  extension_name(x)  extension_description(x)  extension_ctor(x)  new_dock_extensions(x = list())  is_dock_extensions(x)  validate_extensions(x)  as_dock_extensions(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'dock_extensions' as_dock_extensions(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'dock_extension' as_dock_extensions(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'list' as_dock_extensions(x, ...)  extension_block_callback(x, ...)

title: Dock extensions — new_dock_extension
text: server function returning shiny::moduleServer(). Beyond id, called board handles board, update, dock actions, plus extensions – environment exposing every extension's server result keyed ID (carrying state), one extension can read another's state via extensions[[id]]. ui function single argument (ns) returning shiny.tag name Name extension class Extension subclass description Optional free-text description extension, surfaced consumer-neutral metadata (e.g. AI assistant) ctor Constructor function name pkg Package look ctor options Board options supplied extension external_ctrl Set external control (experimental). FALSE (default) opts ; TRUE exposes every constructor input externally controllable; character vector names subset . ... attributes x Extension object id Namespace ID

title: Dock extensions — new_dock_extension
text: constructors new_dock_extension() new_dock_extension(), coercion function as_dock_extension() as_dock_extension(), return objects inherit dock_extension dock_extensions respectively. inheritance structure can checked using is_dock_extension() is_dock_extensions(), return boolean. dock_extension can validated using validate_extension() dock_extensions object using validate_extensions(), return input object invisibly throw errors side-effects. Several getter functions return extension attributes, including extension_ui() (function), extension_server() (function), extension_id() (string), extension_name() (string), extension_description() (string NULL) extension_ctor() (object inherits blockr_ctor).

title: Dock extensions — new_dock_extension
code: ext <- new_edit_board_extension() is_dock_extension(ext) #> [1] TRUE

title: ID utilities — dock_id
text: Objects, blocks dock extensions carry IDs. can converted ID types, panel IDs "handle" IDs. Panel IDs used refer dock panels, handle IDs provide "handles" DOM manipulations. IDs inherit dock_id panel IDs additionally inherit dock_panel_id, handle IDs inherit dock_handle_id. panel IDs, depending whether panel showing block extension, inheritance structure additionally contains block_panel_id ext_panel_id, respectively. Similarly, handle IDs, block_handle_id ext_handle_id block / extension cards, plus view_handle_id view's DOM container. dock_id objects can converted back native IDs, calling as_obj_id(). utility function dock_id() returns (possibly namespaced) ID dock instance used manage visible panels.

title: ID utilities — dock_id
code: dock_id(ns = NULL)  as_dock_panel_id(x)  as_obj_id(x)  as_block_panel_id(x)  as_ext_panel_id(x)  as_dock_handle_id(x)  as_block_handle_id(x)  as_ext_handle_id(x)  as_view_handle_id(x)

title: ID utilities — dock_id
text: ns Namespace prefix x Object

title: ID utilities — dock_id
text: Coercion functions as_block_panel_id(), as_ext_panel_id(), as_block_handle_id() as_ext_handle_id() return objects inherit block_panel_id, ext_panel_id, block_handle_id ext_handle_id classed character vectors. less specific coercion functions as_dock_panel_id() as_dock_handle_id() return objects inherit dock_panel_id dock_handle_id, addition sub-class block_panel_id ext_panel_id (case as_dock_panel_id()). mix sub-classes returned, represented list classed character vectors. as_view_handle_id() maps view id DOM container id (view_handle_id). Finally, as_obj_id() returns character vector, dock_id().

title: ID utilities — dock_id
code: blks <- c(   a = blockr.core::new_dataset_block(),   b = blockr.core::new_head_block() )  ext <- new_edit_board_extension()  as_dock_panel_id(blks) #> [1] "block_panel-a" "block_panel-b" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "block_panel_id" "dock_panel_id"  "dock_id"        as_dock_panel_id(ext) #> [1] "ext_panel-edit_board_extension" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "ext_panel_id"  "dock_panel_id" "dock_id"        identical(names(blks), as_obj_id(as_block_panel_id(blks))) #> [1] TRUE  as_dock_handle_id(blks) #> [1] "block_handle-a" "block_handle-b" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "block_handle_id" "dock_handle_id"  "dock_id"         as_dock_handle_id(ext) #> [1] "ext_handle-edit_board_extension" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "ext_handle_id"  "dock_handle_id" "dock_id"         identical(names(blks), as_obj_id(as_block_handle_id(blks))) #> [1] TRUE

title: Layout serialization and inspection — panel_obj_ids
text: Read write JSON form dock_layout, inspect panel IDs references. canonical accessors serialized layout format — downstream tooling call rather re-implement format.

title: Layout serialization and inspection — panel_obj_ids
code: panel_obj_ids(ids)  layout_panel_ids(layout)  layout_to_json(x, ...)  layout_from_json(x, blocks = NULL, extensions = NULL)

title: Layout serialization and inspection — panel_obj_ids
text: ids Character vector panel IDs. layout dock_layout object. x dock_layout (layout_to_json()), JSON string / parsed spec list (layout_from_json()). ... Forwarded jsonlite::toJSON(). blocks, extensions Optional board components used resolve validate bare IDs layout_from_json().

title: Layout serialization and inspection — panel_obj_ids
text: layout_to_json() returns JSON string; layout_from_json() dock_layout. layout_panel_ids() panel_obj_ids() return character vectors.

title: Layout serialization and inspection — panel_obj_ids
text: layout_to_json() renders layout JSON string; layout_from_json() inverse. shape recursive tree: top object carries orientation, children, optional sizes, optional focus (panel current focus); child either bare string (single-panel leaf), object panels / optional active (tabbed leaf), object children / optional sizes (nested branch). Sizes ratios summing 1, omitted even. layout_from_json() accepts JSON string already-parsed spec list delegates as_dock_layout(); blocks / extensions supplied, bare IDs resolved canonical panel IDs result validated (unknown panel malformed arrangement throws usual classed error). layout_panel_ids() returns canonical panel IDs (block_panel-… / ext_panel-…) referenced layout; panel_obj_ids() strips prefixes back bare block / extension IDs.

title: Layout serialization and inspection — panel_obj_ids
code: ly <- dock_layout("a", panels("b", "c", active = "c"), sizes = c(0.3, 0.7))  json <- layout_to_json(ly) cat(json) #> {"orientation":"horizontal","children":["a",{"panels":["b","c"],"active":"c"}],"sizes":[0.3,0.7]}  identical(layout_from_json(json), ly) #> [1] TRUE

title: Dock layout — dock_layout
text: dock_layout panel arrangement single view: tree block / extension IDs, one leaf marked initially active. board holds dock_layouts collection (one layout per view); panel content derived demand board's blocks extensions, arrangement stored dock_layout. See is_dock_layouts() collection-level helpers.

title: Dock layout — dock_layout
code: dock_layout(   ...,   orientation = c("horizontal", "vertical"),   sizes = NULL,   name = NULL )  panels(..., active = NULL)  group(..., sizes = NULL)  is_dock_layout(x)  as_dock_layout(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'dock_layout' format(x, ..., bare = TRUE)  # S3 method for class 'dock_layout' print(x, ...)  default_layout(blocks, extensions)  validate_dock_layout(x, blocks = character())

title: Dock layout — dock_layout
text: ... dock_layout() group(), layout children (bare IDs, character vectors, lists, panels(), group()). panels(), panel IDs. Otherwise reserved generic consistency. orientation Top-level split direction; one "horizontal" (default) "vertical". sizes Numeric vector parallel ..., giving child's share parent (positive; need sum 1). name dock_layout(), optional display label view (free-form). omitted, label derived view's id. view's id list name new_dock_board(layouts = list(...)), minted absent unique across views dock_layouts. active panels(), ID tab open default. x Object bare format() / print(), drop block_panel- / ext_panel- prefixes panel IDs (see panel_obj_ids()). blocks, extensions Dock board components. default_layout() components arrange; as_dock_layout(), optional, used resolve bare IDs validate result.

title: Dock layout — dock_layout
text: dock_layout() default_layout() return dock_layout object. panels() returns dock_panels node group() returns dock_group node — layout sub-trees usable inside dock_layout() / group(). as_dock_layout() returns dock_layout (board spec list); .list() dock_layout returns spec list. is_dock_layout() returns boolean. validate_dock_layout() returns input throws error.

title: Dock layout — dock_layout
text: Construct layout : dock_layout(...): page-level container. ... children root branch. Bare strings become single-panel leaves, character vectors become tabbed leaves, lists become nested branches. Use panels() tabbed leaf explicit open tab, group() branch explicit sizes. panels(..., active = NULL): tabbed leaf whose tab strip holds given panel IDs. active selects initially-open tab; first ID wins default. single-panel panels() permitted redundant (bare string equivalent). group(..., sizes = NULL): branch container. sizes numeric vector parallel ... overrides even split. default_layout(blocks, extensions) produces default two-row arrangement (extensions top, blocks ) board. dock_layout() accepts orientation = "horizontal" | "vertical" top-level split direction, sizes root-branch ratios, name view's display label. new_dock_board(layouts = list(...)) list name view's stable id (container's key, like block id), minted absent; name sets free-form display label view . name given, one derived id display. view starts active property collection, one layout: pass new_dock_board(active = ) (view id) choose , defaulting first. view conceptual page-level container; layout panel arrangement inside view. dockview-shape grid + panels payload dockViewR consumes internal projection dock_layout board's blocks extensions; public type. as_dock_layout() coerces dock_layout: dock_layout (identity), board (active layout), spec list (.list() layout, parsed layout_to_json() string). Pass blocks / extensions resolve bare IDs canonical panel IDs validate. .list() dock_layout returns spec list. JSON-string boundary layout_to_json() / layout_from_json().

title: Dock layout — dock_layout
code: blks <- c(   a = blockr.core::new_dataset_block(),   b = blockr.core::new_head_block() )  exts <- list(   edit = new_edit_board_extension() )  # The default arrangement for a given set of blocks and extensions default_layout(blks, exts) #> <dock_layout> horizontal #> ├─ edit_board_extension #> └─ tabs #>    ├─ a (active) #>    └─ b  # Tabbed leaf with an explicit open tab panels("a", "b", "edit_board_extension", active = "edit_board_extension") #> $views #> $views[[1]] #> [1] "a" #>  #> $views[[2]] #> [1] "b" #>  #> $views[[3]] #> [1] "edit_board_extension" #>  #>  #> $active #> [1] "edit_board_extension" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "dock_panels" "dock_node"    # Branch with explicit child ratios group("a", "b", sizes = c(0.3, 0.7)) #> $children #> $children[[1]] #> [1] "a" #>  #> $children[[2]] #> [1] "b" #>  #>  #> $sizes #> [1] 0.3 0.7 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "dock_group" "dock_node"   # Composing them inside a layout dock_layout(   "a",   panels("b", "edit_board_extension", active = "edit_board_extension"),   sizes = c(0.3, 0.7) ) #> <dock_layout> horizontal #> ├─ a (30%) #> └─ tabs (70%) #>    ├─ b #>    └─ edit_board_extension (active)  # Vertical top-level split dock_layout("a", "b", orientation = "vertical") #> <dock_layout> vertical #> ├─ a #> └─ b

Inheritance can checked using is_dock_stack(), returns scalar logical color attribute can set retrieved using stack_color<-() (returns modified stack object invisibly) stack_color() (returns string), respectively. Stack objects may coerced "dock_stack" using as_dock_stack() finally, utility function suggest_new_colors() returns character vector new colors, based existing palette.

title: Dock views (layouts) — new_dock_layouts
text: dock_board always holds dock_layouts object (multi-view tabs). Single-page boards degenerate case: one auto-named "Page" view. Blocks extensions shared across views via board's DAG; view membership layout concern .

title: Dock views (layouts) — new_dock_layouts
code: new_dock_layouts(...)  validate_dock_layouts(x)  is_dock_layouts(x)  view_name(x)  view_name(x) <- value  view_names(x)  active_view(x)  active_view(x) <- value  board_layouts(x) <- value  board_layouts(x)  as_dock_layouts(x, ...)

title: Dock views (layouts) — new_dock_layouts
text: ... Generic consistency x dock_layout (view_name() / view_name<-()) dock_layouts collection (view_names()). value Replacement value

title: Dock views (layouts) — new_dock_layouts
text: is_dock_layouts() returns boolean. validate_dock_layouts() returns input throws error. active_view() returns active view's id, NULL view active, active_view<-() returns modified dock_layouts (dock_board) object invisibly. view_name() returns view's explicit display name (NULL), view_name<-() modified dock_layout, view_names() character vector display labels keyed view id (derived id view explicit name).

title: Dock views (layouts) — new_dock_layouts
text: view carries stable, immutable id (key dock_layouts collection) distinct editable display name. mirrors id / name separation used blocks (immutable id keys collection; blockr.core::block_name() editable label). id minted view created never changes — rename rewrites name attribute, never key. Use view_name() / view_name<-() read write view's display name view_names() names collection. Multi-view boards defined passing named list new_dock_board()'s layouts argument: name view's id (container's key, like block id — minted absent), value panel arrangement inside view (dock_layout(), raw list block / extension IDs). display name set layout via dock_layout(name = ); none set, label derived id. initially-active view chosen new_dock_board(active = ) (view id), defaulting first; property collection, never individual layout. View CRUD enabled unless dock locked (see is_dock_locked()). Users normally construct dock_layouts directly; instead pass plain named list new_dock_board(layouts = ...), resolves, validates wraps (minting absent ids).

title: Dock views (layouts) — new_dock_layouts
code: brd <- new_dock_board(   blocks = c(     dataset_1 = blockr.core::new_dataset_block(),     head_1 = blockr.core::new_head_block()   ),   layouts = list(     analysis = dock_layout("dataset_1", "head_1", name = "Analysis"),     overview = dock_layout("dataset_1", name = "Overview")   ),   active = "overview" ) view_names(board_layouts(brd)) #>   analysis   overview  #> "Analysis" "Overview"

title: blockr.dock (development version)
text: Adding block dock view finished initialising longer throws argument length zero. dock uninitialised layout NULL; determine_active_views() now treats empty dock, block’s panel placed freely instead stranded offcanvas. add_link_action() now mounts blockr.ui link-menu module bidirectional: right-clicking downstream block now lets pick upstream source, just target. handler passes board anchor reactives, menu owns link-id validation keeps pinned menu sync board - removing link frees target whose card reappears live, removing block drops card, without re-render. per-field link inputs (create_link / add_link_input / add_link_id / add_link_confirm) dock-side valid_link_id validator gone favour single committed-spec reactive blockr.ui::link_menu_server(). link_sidebar_body() removed (-tree callers remain; --tree consumers migrate blockr.ui::link_menu_ui() / link_menu_server()). add / edit stack action handlers now mount blockr.ui stack-menu module: multi-select card-list block picker search, per-category icons, inline hue / lightness colour picker, panel-level form stack name / colour / id. per-field Shiny inputs (stack_id / stack_name / stack_color / stack_block_selection / stack_confirm edit_stack_* equivalents) gone favour single committed-stack reactive returned blockr.ui::stack_menu_server(). stack_sidebar_body() removed (-tree callers remain; --tree consumers migrate blockr.ui::stack_menu_ui()). Drops shinyWidgets::colorPickr floating popover; new colour picker renders inline sidebar. handlers now pass board reactive, pinned stack menu stays sync board changes (removing block drops card live); spec validation moved blockr.ui (dock-side valid_stack_* validators gone). menu builds dock_stack objects via blockr.dock::new_dock_stack() (gated behind pkg_avail("blockr.dock"), Suggests back-edge), handlers apply committed stacks object -. Layout deserialization now routes producing blockr.dock version rather sniffing payload shape. blockr_deser.dock_board() reads producer version saved constructor$version threads (blockr.core’s ...-forwarding blockr_deser.list()) blockr_deser.dock_layout(), picks wire-format reader version-keyed registry. Shape discrimination (legacy dockview grid vs. flattened spec) stays fallback version-less payloads — old saves hand-crafted JSON (#153). dock “manager” object gone. apply_board_update.dock_board() now pure reducer board_layouts(); live view surgery (instantiate / tear / restore / rename / switch) runs single closure-resident reconcile pass driven committed board, replacing duplicated delta-driven UI-driven view CRUD. View init just empty-registry case pass (create every view, show active one), separate init path. per-session dock state ordinary closure-private state passed explicitly, handle threaded back board callback dot_args. Also makes augment_board_update.dock_board() idempotent — view id minted rather re-minted every augment pass — fixing view-add loop (#164). Views now carry stable, immutable id decoupled editable display name, mirroring id / name split used blocks. dock_layouts (runtime dock_mgr$docks registry) keyed id; name attribute read / written via view_name() / view_name<-() (view_names() whole collection), active_view() now returns active view’s id. Renaming view pure name-attribute write — id, dock module DOM element untouched, structure ever re-keyed (live-sync rename longer leaks remove--add). dock module / DOM ids derive deterministically view id (random per-render minting), views delta gains rename slot. Naming constraints relax display concerns (non-empty, unique label). Serialization round-trips ids. new_dock_board(layouts = list(...)) list name view’s id (container’s key, like block id — minted absent); display name set view via dock_layout(name = ) falls back label derived id unset. views delta addresses existing views id (stable handle) — mod / rm / active carry ids; add supplies display name mints id. Producers addressed views name (e.g. blockr.assistant) must switch ids (#166). dock_layout objects gain format() / print() methods render arrangement indented tree: orientation, nested groups sizes, tabbed leaves active tab, focused panel. Panel IDs print without block_panel- / ext_panel- prefixes default; pass bare = FALSE canonical IDs (#161). add / append / prepend block action handlers now mount blockr.ui block-browser module: card-list block picker search, per-category icons, per-card expand tweaking id / title / link / port adding. Repeated single clicks card produce distinct blocks (suggested ids seeded board never collide), replacing old single-select selectize form. per-field Shiny inputs (<mode>_block_selection / <mode>_block_id / <mode>_block_name / <mode>_link_id / <mode>_block_input / <mode>_block_confirm) gone favour single committed-block reactive returned blockr.ui::block_browser_server(). block_sidebar_body() removed (nothing -tree calls ; link / stack flows use link_sidebar_body() / stack_sidebar_body() unchanged). Requires blockr.ui block-browser module.

title: blockr.dock 0.1.2
text: views slot board_update payload now structured delta (add / mod / rm / active) instead wholesale dock_layouts replacement. Mentioned views touched, omitted views keep current state, four sub-slots compose atomically blocks / links / stacks lifecycle tick. See ?dock_board_update_lifecycle contract (#150). Removing block longer clears active view’s layout. Instead, every view containing removed block block’s panel dropped surgically layout, preserving rest grid. augment_board_update.dock_board() performs cleanup update() lifecycle path; rm_blocks.dock_board() surgically (rather nuking active layout) direct callers like clear_board() (#150). board_layouts(rv$board) now stays sync UI-driven layout changes (panel close/add, drag-resize/rearrange, view CRUD). UI-driven mutations routed update(list(views = ...)) applied via validate_board_update.dock_board() apply_board_update.dock_board(). Writes debounced (250 ms) drag-resize doesn’t thrash. Requires blockr.core (>= 0.1.3) update lifecycle generics.

title: blockr.dock 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-29 Added prepend block action. Define multi-view boards passing named list new_dock_board(layouts = ...): Mark arrangement initially active dock_layout(..., active = TRUE): none marked, first one used. board’s initialise_layout normalises slot dock_layout (storing arrangement ), stores result dock_layouts collection. Breaking changes layout API: Renamed new_dock_board()’s layout parameter layouts (corresponding board field), since boards now hold dock_layouts collection. Renamed board accessor dock_layout(brd) (setter dock_layout(brd) <-) active_layout(brd) / active_layout(brd) <-. Renamed board_views(brd) board_layouts(brd), corresponding setter dock_layouts(brd) <- value board_layouts(brd) <- value. dock_layout now per-view arrangement type. dock_layout(...) constructs one nested list block / extension IDs accepts orientation, sizes, active arguments. previously-exported “fully-resolved” dock_layout (grid + panels wire shape) gone — panel content derived board’s blocks extensions demand dockview boundary, per-view storage shrinks just arrangement panel definitions longer duplicate across views. Added panels(..., active = NULL) tabbed leaves explicit open tab, group(..., sizes = NULL) nested branches explicit ratios. dock_layout() also accepts sizes = root-level ratios orientation = top-level split direction. Removed dock_view(), dock_grid(), is_dock_grid(), as_dock_grid(). Use dock_layout(...) (new panels() / group()) per-view spec. Renamed default_layout() → kept name default_layout(). now returns dock_layout (arrangement ) — previously-exported default_grid() (panel-ID form) gone. Unexported high-level resolver create_dock_layout() (renamed internally resolve_dock_layout()). Unexported dock_layouts() constructor. user-facing input shape new_dock_board(layouts = ...) plain named list — dock_layouts type resolved collection board holds internally. is_dock_layouts(), as_dock_layouts(), validate_dock_layouts() remain exported. Unexported new_dock_layout(); use dock_layout() instead. Unexported view_ids() view_can_crud(). internal helpers exposed accident; renamed layout_ids() views_can_crud() respectively align operate . Wire format serialised dock_layout decoupled dockview’s internal tree. persisted shape flattened recursive spec: top object carries orientation, children, optional sizes, optional focus (panel current focus); child bare string (single-panel leaf), {panels, active?} object (tabbed leaf), {children, sizes?} object (nested branch). Sizes ratios (auto-normalised dockview’s pixel sizes); even splits omit sizes; default open tab omits active; focus first leaf omits focus. focus round-trips focused group (dockview’s activeGroup) naming stable panel rather regenerated group id. Legacy payloads (dockview-shape grid field) load via shape-discriminated reader. Producer-version routing tracked #153 (depends blockr.core forwarding ... blockr_deser.list). Layout conversion API split boundary. R object ↔︎ R list boundary uses coercion: as_dock_layout() coerces dock_layout (identity), board (active layout), spec list dock_layout; .list() dock_layout returns spec list. R object ↔︎ JSON string boundary uses explicit verbs: layout_to_json() / layout_from_json(). as_dock_layout(<list>) layout_from_json() take optional blocks / extensions resolve bare IDs validate. layout_panel_ids() / panel_obj_ids() inspect panel / object IDs layout references. dockview wire format converters part public API — dock_layout object, JSON, spec list ; as_dock_layout() rejects dockview grid-shaped list.
code: layouts = list(   Analysis = list("block_1", "block_2"),   Overview = list("dag_extension") ) layouts = list(   Analysis = list("block_1", "block_2"),   Overview = dock_layout("dag_extension", active = TRUE) )

title: blockr.dock 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-11 Initial CRAN submission
